questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In a radioactive decay reaction:
If the sample has only $A$ initially, then select correct alternative(s) at the instant the number of the particles of $B$ is maximum:
options: Activity of $A$ is equal to activity of $B$
No of atoms of $A$ is 4.5 times of $B$
Activity of A is more than activity of B
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{d[A]}{d t}=-2 \lambda N_A \\ & \frac{d[B]}{d t}=2 \lambda N_A-9 \lambda N B\end{aligned}$
At the time $B$ is maximum, $\frac{d[B]}{d t}=0$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=4.5 \mathrm{~N}_{\mathrm{B}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Assume that the nuclear binding energy per nucleon (B/A) versus mass number (A) is as shown in the figure. Consider a nucleus of $A=110$. Fission of this nucleus results into 2 fragments. Which of the following could possibly be the mass number of the resulting nuclei?
questions.solution:
Fission of a nucleus is feasible only if the binding energy of daughter nuclei is more than the parent nucleus.
$A=55$ will have more $B E$ than 110 .
$A=70$ will have same $B E$ as 110 but $A=40$ will have more B.E.
$A=100$ will have same $B E$ as 110 but $A=10$ will have lesser B.E.
$A=90$ will have same $B E$ as 110 but $A=20$ will have lesser B.E.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a radioactive sample having equal number of radio active nuclei $A$ and $B$. A decays in $X$ with decay constant $\lambda$. B decay in $X$ and $Y$ with decay constant $\lambda$ and $2 \lambda$ respectively. Initially number of nuclei are given as $N_A=N_B=N_0$ and $N_x=N_y=0$. At any later instant $t$, select the correct alternative(s) regarding number of nuclei of $A, B, X$ and $Y$.
questions.solution: For, $A \longrightarrow N_A=N_0 e^{-\lambda t}$ $\begin{aligned} & \quad B \longrightarrow \lambda_{\text {eff }}=\lambda+2 \lambda=3 \lambda \\ & N_B=N_0 e^{-3 \lambda t} \\ & \text { At } \rightarrow \infty \\ & N_x+N_y=N_0 \\ & \frac{N_x}{N_y}=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow N_x=\frac{N_0}{3}\left[1-e^{-3 \lambda t}\right] \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The potential difference applied to an X-ray tube is increased. Which of the following statement is tr about the radiation emitted?
options: $\text { Minimum wavelength of the radiation increases }$
$\text { Intensity of the radiation remains unchanged. }$
$\text { Minimum wavelength of the radiation decreases. }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
